mnemonics: A Private Key is like your Password (PRP), A Public Key is like your Username (PUB). Your Password is Private, your Username is Public.
keyPoints: Public key cryptography uses a mathematically linked pair of keys: a public key (shared openly) and a private key (kept secret).
Data encrypted with the public key can only be decrypted by the corresponding private key, enabling secure communication.
Digital signatures are created by encrypting a hash with a private key, which can be verified by anyone with the public key, proving authenticity and integrity.
commonMistakes: Confusing the roles of the keys, e.g., trying to decrypt a message with the same public key that was used to encrypt it.
Believing that public key encryption is a replacement for all encryption needs, when it is often slower and used primarily for securely exchanging symmetric keys.
